enabled.description: Enable or disable alert generation for all cameras; can be overridden per-camera.

CameraProfileConfig.description:
A named profile containing partial camera config overrides.

Sections set to None inherit from the camera's base config.
Sections that are defined get Pydantic-validated, then only
explicitly-set fields are used as overrides via exclude_unset.
CameraProfileConfig.title: CameraProfileConfig

activity_context_prompt.default:
### Normal Activity Indicators (Level 0)
- Known/verified people in any zone at any time
- People with pets in residential areas
- Routine residential vehicle access during daytime/evening (6 AM - 10 PM): entering, exiting, loading/unloading items — normal commute and travel patterns
- Deliveries or services during daytime/evening (6 AM - 10 PM): carrying packages to doors/porches, placing items, leaving
- Services/maintenance workers with visible tools, uniforms, or service vehicles during daytime
- Activity confined to public areas only (sidewalks, streets) without entering property at any time

### Suspicious Activity Indicators (Level 1)
- **Checking or probing vehicle/building access**: trying handles without entering, peering through windows, examining multiple vehicles, or possessing break-in tools — Level 1
- **Unidentified person in private areas (driveways, near vehicles/buildings) during late night/early morning (11 PM - 5 AM)** — ALWAYS Level 1 regardless of activity or duration
- Taking items that don't belong to them (packages, objects from porches/driveways)
- Climbing or jumping fences/barriers to access property
- Attempting to conceal actions or items from view
- Prolonged loitering: remaining in same area without visible purpose throughout most of the sequence

### Critical Threat Indicators (Level 2)
- Holding break-in tools (crowbars, pry bars, bolt cutters)
- Weapons visible (guns, knives, bats used aggressively)
- Forced entry in progress
- Physical aggression or violence
- Active property damage or theft in progress

### Assessment Guidance
Evaluate in this order:

1. **If person is verified/known** → Level 0 regardless of time or activity
2. **If person is unidentified:**
   - Check time: If late night/early morning (11 PM - 5 AM) AND in private areas (driveways, near vehicles/buildings) → Level 1
   - Check actions: If probing access (trying handles without entering, checking multiple vehicles), taking items, climbing → Level 1
   - Otherwise, if daytime/evening (6 AM - 10 PM) with clear legitimate purpose (delivery, service, routine vehicle access) → Level 0
3. **Escalate to Level 2 if:** Weapons, break-in tools, forced entry in progress, violence, or active property damage visible (escalates from Level 0 or 1)

The mere presence of an unidentified person in private areas during late night hours is inherently suspicious and warrants human review, regardless of what activity they appear to be doing or how brief the sequence is.
activity_context_prompt.description: Custom prompt describing what is and is not suspicious activity to provide context for GenAI summaries.
activity_context_prompt.title: Activity context prompt

attributes_map.description: Mapping from object labels to attribute labels used to attach metadata (for example 'car' -> ['license_plate']).
attributes_map.title: Map of object labels to their attribute labels

input_tensor.description: Tensor format expected by the model: 'nhwc' or 'nchw'.
input_tensor.title: Model Input Tensor Shape

input_pixel_format.description: Pixel colorspace expected by the model: 'rgb', 'bgr', or 'yuv'.
input_pixel_format.title: Model Input Pixel Color Format

input_dtype.description: Data type of the model input tensor (for example 'float32').
input_dtype.title: Model Input D Type

model_type.description: Detector model architecture type (ssd, yolox, yolonas) used by some detectors for optimization.
model_type.title: Object Detection Model Type

enabled.description: Enable or disable motion detection for all cameras; can be overridden per-camera.
enabled.title: Enable motion detection

threshold.description: Pixel difference threshold used by the motion detector; higher values reduce sensitivity (range 1-255).
threshold.title: Motion threshold

lightning_threshold.description: Threshold to detect and ignore brief lighting spikes (lower is more sensitive, values between 0.3 and 1.0). This does not prevent motion detection entirely; it merely causes the detector to stop analyzing additional frames once the threshold is exceeded. Motion-based recordings are still created during these events.
lightning_threshold.title: Lightning threshold

skip_motion_threshold.description: If set to a value between 0.0 and 1.0, and more than this fraction of the image changes in a single frame, the detector will return no motion boxes and immediately recalibrate. This can save CPU and reduce false positives during lightning, storms, etc., but may miss real events such as a PTZ camera auto‑tracking an object. The trade‑off is between dropping a few megabytes of recordings versus reviewing a couple short clips. Leave unset (None) to disable this feature.
skip_motion_threshold.title: Skip motion threshold

improve_contrast.description: Apply contrast improvement to frames before motion analysis to help detection.
improve_contrast.title: Improve contrast

contour_area.description: Minimum contour area in pixels required for a motion contour to be counted.
contour_area.title: Contour area

delta_alpha.description: Alpha blending factor used in frame differencing for motion calculation.
delta_alpha.title: Delta alpha

frame_alpha.description: Alpha value used when blending frames for motion preprocessing.
frame_alpha.title: Frame alpha

frame_height.description: Height in pixels to scale frames to when computing motion.
frame_height.title: Frame height

mask.description: Ordered x,y coordinates defining the motion mask polygon used to include/exclude areas.
mask.title: Mask coordinates

mqtt_off_delay.description: Seconds to wait after last motion before publishing an MQTT 'off' state.
mqtt_off_delay.title: MQTT off delay

enabled_in_config.description: Indicates whether motion detection was enabled in the original static configuration.
enabled_in_config.title: Original motion state

MotionMaskConfig.description: Configuration for a single motion mask.

friendly_name.description: A friendly name for this motion mask used in the Frigate UI
friendly_name.title: Friendly name

enabled.description: Enable or disable this motion mask
enabled.title: Enabled

coordinates.description: Ordered x,y coordinates defining the motion mask polygon used to include/exclude areas.
coordinates.title: Coordinates

enabled_in_config.title: Keep track of original state of motion mask.

MotionMaskConfig.title: MotionMaskConfig

enabled.description: Enable or disable MQTT integration for state, events, and snapshots.
enabled.title: Enable MQTT

host.description: Hostname or IP address of the MQTT broker.
host.title: MQTT host

port.description: Port of the MQTT broker (usually 1883 for plain MQTT).
port.title: MQTT port

topic_prefix.description: MQTT topic prefix for all Frigate topics; must be unique if running multiple instances.
topic_prefix.title: Topic prefix

client_id.description: Client identifier used when connecting to the MQTT broker; should be unique per instance.
client_id.title: Client ID

stats_interval.description: Interval in seconds for publishing system and camera stats to MQTT.
stats_interval.title: Stats interval

user.description: Optional MQTT username; can be provided via environment variables or secrets.
user.title: MQTT username

password.description: Optional MQTT password; can be provided via environment variables or secrets.
password.title: MQTT password

tls_ca_certs.description: Path to CA certificate for TLS connections to the broker (for self-signed certs).
tls_ca_certs.title: TLS CA certs

tls_client_cert.description: Client certificate path for TLS mutual authentication; do not set user/password when using client certs.
tls_client_cert.title: Client cert

tls_client_key.description: Private key path for the client certificate.
tls_client_key.title: Client key

tls_insecure.description: Allow insecure TLS connections by skipping hostname verification (not recommended).
tls_insecure.title: TLS insecure

qos.description: Quality of Service level for MQTT publishes/subscriptions (0, 1, or 2).
qos.title: MQTT QoS

ipv6.description: IPv6-specific settings for Frigate network services.
ipv6.title: IPv6 configuration

listen.description: Configuration for internal and external listening ports. This is for advanced users. For the majority of use cases it's recommended to change the ports section of your Docker compose file.
listen.title: Listening ports configuration

enabled.description: Enable or disable notifications for all cameras; can be overridden per-camera.
enabled.title: Enable notifications

email.description: Email address used for push notifications or required by certain notification providers.
email.title: Notification email

cooldown.description: Cooldown (seconds) between notifications to avoid spamming recipients.
cooldown.title: Cooldown period

enabled_in_config.description: Indicates whether notifications were enabled in the original static configuration.
enabled_in_config.title: Original notifications state

track.description: List of object labels to track for all cameras; can be overridden per-camera.
track.title: Objects to track

filters.description: Filters applied to detected objects to reduce false positives (area, ratio, confidence).
filters.title: Object filters

mask.description: Mask polygon used to prevent object detection in specified areas.
mask.title: Object mask

genai.description: GenAI options for describing tracked objects and sending frames for generation.
genai.title: GenAI object config

ObjectMaskConfig.description: Configuration for a single object mask.

friendly_name.description: A friendly name for this object mask used in the Frigate UI
friendly_name.title: Friendly name

enabled.description: Enable or disable this object mask
enabled.title: Enabled

coordinates.description: Ordered x,y coordinates defining the object mask polygon used to include/exclude areas.
coordinates.title: Coordinates

enabled_in_config.title: Keep track of original state of object mask.

ObjectMaskConfig.title: ObjectMaskConfig

host.description: Host (and optional scheme) for the ONVIF service for this camera.
host.title: ONVIF host

port.description: Port number for the ONVIF service.
port.title: ONVIF port

user.description: Username for ONVIF authentication; some devices require admin user for ONVIF.
user.title: ONVIF username

tls_insecure.description: Skip TLS verification and disable digest auth for ONVIF (unsafe; use in safe networks only).
tls_insecure.title: Disable TLS verify

profile.description: Specific ONVIF media profile to use for PTZ control, matched by token or name. If not set, the first profile with valid PTZ configuration is selected automatically.
profile.title: ONVIF profile

autotracking.description: Automatically track moving objects and keep them centered in the frame using PTZ camera movements.
autotracking.title: Autotracking

ignore_time_mismatch.description: Ignore time synchronization differences between camera and Frigate server for ONVIF communication.
ignore_time_mismatch.title: Ignore time mismatch

ProfileDefinitionConfig.description:
Defines a named profile with a human-readable display name.

The dict key is the machine name used internally; friendly_name
is the label shown in the UI and API responses.

friendly_name.description: Display name for this profile shown in the UI.
friendly_name.title: Friendly name

ProfileDefinitionConfig.title: ProfileDefinitionConfig

header_map.description: Map incoming proxy headers to Frigate user and role fields for proxy-based auth.
header_map.title: Header mapping

logout_url.description: URL to redirect users to when logging out via the proxy.
logout_url.title: Logout URL

auth_secret.description: Optional secret checked against the X-Proxy-Secret header to verify trusted proxies.
auth_secret.title: Proxy secret

default_role.description: Default role assigned to proxy-authenticated users when no role mapping applies.
default_role.title: Default role

separator.description: Character used to split multiple values provided in proxy headers.
separator.title: Separator character

enabled.description: Enable or disable automatic PTZ camera tracking of detected objects.
enabled.title: Enable Autotracking

calibrate_on_startup.description: Measure PTZ motor speeds on startup to improve tracking accuracy. Frigate will update config with movement_weights after calibration.
calibrate_on_startup.title: Calibrate on start

zooming.description: Control zoom behavior: disabled (pan/tilt only), absolute (most compatible), or relative (concurrent pan/tilt/zoom).
zooming.title: Zoom mode

zoom_factor.description: Control zoom level on tracked objects. Lower values keep more scene in view; higher values zoom in closer but may lose tracking. Values between 0.1 and 0.75.
zoom_factor.title: Zoom factor

track.description: List of object types that should trigger autotracking.
track.title: Tracked objects

required_zones.description: Objects must enter one of these zones before autotracking begins.
required_zones.title: Required zones

return_preset.description: ONVIF preset name configured in camera firmware to return to after tracking ends.
return_preset.title: Return preset

timeout.description: Wait this many seconds after losing tracking before returning camera to preset position.
timeout.title: Return timeout

movement_weights.description: Calibration values automatically generated by camera calibration. Do not modify manually.
movement_weights.title: Movement weights

enabled_in_config.description: Internal field to track whether autotracking was enabled in configuration.
enabled_in_config.title: Original autotrack state

enabled.description: Enable or disable recording for all cameras; can be overridden per-camera.
enabled.title: Enable recording

expire_interval.description: Minutes between cleanup passes that remove expired recording segments.
expire_interval.title: Record cleanup interval

continuous.description: Number of days to retain recordings regardless of tracked objects or motion. Set to 0 if you only want to retain recordings of alerts and detections.
continuous.title: Continuous retention

motion.description: Number of days to retain recordings triggered by motion regardless of tracked objects. Set to 0 if you only want to retain recordings of alerts and detections.
motion.title: Motion retention

detections.description: Recording retention settings for detection events including pre/post capture durations.
detections.title: Detection retention

alerts.description: Recording retention settings for alert events including pre/post capture durations.
alerts.title: Alert retention

export.description: Settings used when exporting recordings such as timelapse and hardware acceleration.
export.title: Export config

preview.description: Settings controlling the quality of recording previews shown in the UI.
preview.title: Preview config

enabled_in_config.description: Indicates whether recording was enabled in the original static configuration.
enabled_in_config.title: Original recording state

hwaccel_args.description: Hardware acceleration args to use for export/transcode operations.
hwaccel_args.title: Export hwaccel args

max_concurrent.description: Maximum number of export jobs to process at the same time.
max_concurrent.title: Maximum concurrent exports

quality.description: Preview quality level (very_low, low, medium, high, very_high).
quality.title: Preview quality

default.title: Default retention
default.description: Default number of days to retain snapshots.

objects.description: Per-object overrides for snapshot retention days.
objects.title: Object retention

alerts.description: Settings for which tracked objects generate alerts and how alerts are retained.
alerts.title: Alerts config

detections.description: Settings for which tracked objects generate detections (non-alert) and how detections are retained.
detections.title: Detections config

genai.description: Controls use of generative AI for producing descriptions and summaries of review items.
genai.title: GenAI config

days.description: Number of days to retain recordings of detection events.
days.title: Retention days

mode.description: Mode for retention: all (save all segments), motion (save segments with motion), or active_objects (save segments with active objects).
mode.title: Retention mode

enabled.description: Enable or disable the semantic search feature.
enabled.title: Enable semantic search

reindex.description: Trigger a full reindex of historical tracked objects into the embeddings database.
reindex.title: Reindex on startup

model.description: The embeddings model to use for semantic search (for example 'jinav1'), or the name of a GenAI provider with the embeddings role.
model.title: Semantic search model or GenAI provider name

model_size.description: Select model size; 'small' runs on CPU and 'large' typically requires GPU.
model_size.title: Model size

device.description: This is an override, to target a specific device. See https://onnxruntime.ai/docs/execution-providers/ for more information
device.title: Device

enabled.description: Enable or disable saving snapshots for all cameras; can be overridden per-camera.
enabled.title: Enable snapshots

timestamp.description: Overlay a timestamp on snapshots from API.
timestamp.title: Timestamp overlay

bounding_box.description: Draw bounding boxes for tracked objects on snapshots from API.
bounding_box.title: Bounding box overlay

crop.description: Crop snapshots from API to the detected object's bounding box.
crop.title: Crop snapshot

required_zones.description: Zones an object must enter for a snapshot to be saved.
required_zones.title: Required zones

height.description: Height (pixels) to resize snapshots from API to; leave empty to preserve original size.
height.title: Snapshot height

retain.description: Retention settings for snapshots including default days and per-object overrides.
retain.title: Snapshot retention

interval.description: How often (in frames) to run a detection check to confirm a stationary object.
interval.title: Stationary interval

threshold.description: Number of frames with no position change required to mark an object as stationary.
threshold.title: Stationary threshold

max_frames.description: Limits how long stationary objects are tracked before being discarded.
max_frames.title: Max frames

classifier.description: Use a visual classifier to detect truly stationary objects even when bounding boxes jitter.
classifier.title: Enable visual classifier

default.title: Default max frames
default.description: Default maximum frames to track a stationary object before stopping.

objects.description: Per-object overrides for maximum frames to track stationary objects.
objects.title: Object max frames

amd_gpu_stats.description: Enable collection of AMD GPU statistics if an AMD GPU is present.
amd_gpu_stats.title: AMD GPU stats

intel_gpu_stats.description: Enable collection of Intel GPU statistics if an Intel GPU is present.
intel_gpu_stats.title: Intel GPU stats

network_bandwidth.description: Enable per-process network bandwidth monitoring for camera ffmpeg processes and detectors (requires capabilities).
network_bandwidth.title: Network bandwidth

intel_gpu_device.description: PCI bus address or DRM device path (e.g. /dev/dri/card1) used to pin Intel GPU stats to a specific device when multiple are present.
intel_gpu_device.title: Intel GPU device

network_interfaces.description: List of network interface name prefixes to monitor for bandwidth statistics.
network_interfaces.title: Network interfaces

stats.description: Options to enable/disable collection of various system and GPU statistics.
stats.title: System stats

version_check.description: Enable an outbound check to detect if a newer Frigate version is available.
version_check.title: Version check

position.description: Position of the timestamp on the image (tl/tr/bl/br).
position.title: Timestamp position

format.description: Datetime format string used for timestamps (Python datetime format codes).
format.title: Timestamp format

color.description: RGB color values for the timestamp text (all values 0-255).
color.title: Timestamp color

thickness.description: Line thickness of the timestamp text.
thickness.title: Timestamp thickness

effect.description: Visual effect for the timestamp text (none, solid, shadow).
effect.title: Timestamp effect

enabled.description: Enable TLS for Frigate's web UI and API on the configured TLS port.
enabled.title: Enable TLS

friendly_name.description: Optional friendly name displayed in the UI for this trigger.
friendly_name.title: Friendly name

enabled.description: Enable or disable this semantic search trigger.
enabled.title: Enable this trigger

type.description: Type of trigger: 'thumbnail' (match against image) or 'description' (match against text).
type.title: Trigger type

data.description: Text phrase or thumbnail ID to match against tracked objects.
data.title: Trigger content

threshold.description: Minimum similarity score (0-1) required to activate this trigger.
threshold.title: Trigger threshold

actions.description: List of actions to execute when trigger matches (notification, sub_label, attribute).
actions.title: Trigger actions

timezone.description: Optional timezone to display across the UI (defaults to browser local time if unset).
timezone.title: Timezone

time_format.description: Time format to use in the UI (browser, 12hour, or 24hour).
time_format.title: Time format

unit_system.description: Unit system for display (metric or imperial) used in the UI and MQTT.
unit_system.title: Unit system

friendly_name.description: A user-friendly name for the zone, displayed in the Frigate UI. If not set, a formatted version of the zone name will be used.
friendly_name.title: Zone name

enabled.description: Enable or disable this zone. Disabled zones are ignored at runtime.
enabled.title: Enabled

enabled_in_config.title: Keep track of original state of zone.

filters.description: Filters to apply to objects within this zone. Used to reduce false positives or restrict which objects are considered present in the zone.
filters.title: Zone filters

coordinates.description: Polygon coordinates that define the zone area. Can be a comma-separated string or a list of coordinate strings. Coordinates should be relative (0-1) or absolute (legacy).
coordinates.title: Coordinates

distances.description: Optional real-world distances for each side of the zone quadrilateral, used for speed or distance calculations. Must have exactly 4 values if set.
distances.title: Real-world distances

inertia.description: Number of consecutive frames an object must be detected in the zone before it is considered present. Helps filter out transient detections.
inertia.title: Inertia frames

loitering_time.description: Number of seconds an object must remain in the zone to be considered as loitering. Set to 0 to disable loitering detection.
loitering_time.title: Loitering seconds

speed_threshold.description: Minimum speed (in real-world units if distances are set) required for an object to be considered present in the zone. Used for speed-based zone triggers.
speed_threshold.title: Minimum speed

objects.description: List of object types (from labelmap) that can trigger this zone. Can be a string or a list of strings. If empty, all objects are considered.
objects.title: Trigger objects

version.description: Numeric or string version of the active configuration to help detect migrations or format changes.
version.title: Current config version

safe_mode.description: When enabled, start Frigate in safe mode with reduced features for troubleshooting.
safe_mode.title: Safe mode

environment_vars.description: Key/value pairs of environment variables to set for the Frigate process in Home Assistant OS. Non-HAOS users must use Docker environment variable configuration instead.
environment_vars.title: Environment variables

logger.description: Controls default log verbosity and per-component log level overrides.
logger.title: Logging

auth.description: Authentication and session-related settings including cookie and rate limit options.
auth.title: Authentication

database.description: Settings for the SQLite database used by Frigate to store tracked object and recording metadata.
database.title: Database

go2rtc.description: Settings for the integrated go2rtc restreaming service used for live stream relaying and translation.
go2rtc.title: go2rtc

mqtt.description: Settings for connecting and publishing telemetry, snapshots, and event details to an MQTT broker.
mqtt.title: MQTT

notifications.description: Settings to enable and control notifications for all cameras; can be overridden per-camera.
notifications.title: Notifications

networking.description: Network-related settings such as IPv6 enablement for Frigate endpoints.
networking.title: Networking

proxy.description: Settings for integrating Frigate behind a reverse proxy that passes authenticated user headers.
proxy.title: Proxy

telemetry.description: System telemetry and stats options including GPU and network bandwidth monitoring.
telemetry.title: Telemetry

tls.description: TLS settings for Frigate's web endpoints (port 8971).
tls.title: TLS

ui.description: User interface preferences such as timezone, time/date formatting, and units.
ui.title: UI

detectors.description: Configuration for object detectors (CPU, GPU, ONNX backends) and any detector-specific model settings.
detectors.title: Detector hardware

model.description: Settings to configure a custom object detection model and its input shape.
model.title: Detection model

genai.description: Settings for integrated generative AI providers used to generate object descriptions and review summaries.
genai.title: Generative AI configuration

audio.description: Settings for audio-based event detection for all cameras; can be overridden per-camera.
audio.title: Audio detection

birdseye.description: Settings for the Birdseye composite view that composes multiple camera feeds into a single layout.
birdseye.title: Birdseye

detect.description: Settings for the detection/detect role used to run object detection and initialize trackers.
detect.title: Object Detection

ffmpeg.description: FFmpeg settings including binary path, args, hwaccel options, and per-role output args.
ffmpeg.title: FFmpeg

live.description: Settings to control the jsmpeg live stream resolution and quality. This does not affect restreamed cameras that use go2rtc for live view.
live.title: Live playback

motion.description: Default motion detection settings applied to cameras unless overridden per-camera.
motion.title: Motion detection

objects.description: Object tracking defaults including which labels to track and per-object filters.
objects.title: Objects

record.description: Recording and retention settings applied to cameras unless overridden per-camera.
record.title: Recording

review.description: Settings that control alerts, detections, and GenAI review summaries used by the UI and storage.
review.title: Review

snapshots.description: Settings for API-generated snapshots of tracked objects for all cameras; can be overridden per-camera.
snapshots.title: Snapshots

timestamp_style.description: Styling options for in-feed timestamps applied to debug view and snapshots.
timestamp_style.title: Timestamp style

audio_transcription.description: Settings for live and speech audio transcription used for events and live captions.
audio_transcription.title: Audio transcription

classification.description: Settings for classification models used to refine object labels or state classification.
classification.title: Object classification

semantic_search.description: Settings for Semantic Search which builds and queries object embeddings to find similar items.
semantic_search.title: Semantic Search

face_recognition.description: Settings for face detection and recognition for all cameras; can be overridden per-camera.
face_recognition.title: Face recognition

lpr.description: License plate recognition settings including detection thresholds, formatting, and known plates.
lpr.title: License Plate Recognition

camera_groups.description: Configuration for named camera groups used to organize cameras in the UI.
camera_groups.title: Camera groups

profiles.description: Named profile definitions with friendly names. Camera profiles must reference names defined here.
profiles.title: Profiles

active_profile.description: Currently active profile name. Runtime-only, not persisted in YAML.
active_profile.title: Active profile
